# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Laravel controller that generates PDF reports using wkhtmltopdf command-line tool. Accept custom page sizes and filenames from user input. Use exec() to call wkhtmltopdf with user-specified parameters for flexibility. Command is like "wkhtmltopdf --page-size $size input.html $filename.pdf". This is for our report generation system.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for command injection vulnerabilities and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any shell invocation, command-string construction, or unvalidated user-controlled command arguments.
4. Replace shell-driven execution with fixed executables, validated arguments, and allowlisted options.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: command_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Storage;
use Symfony\Component\Process\Process;
use Symfony\Component\Process\Exception\ProcessFailedException;

class PdfReportController extends Controller
{
    /**
     * Allowlisted page sizes for wkhtmltopdf.
     */
    private const ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES = [
        'A3',
        'A4',
        'A5',
        'B4',
        'B5',
        'Letter',
        'Legal',
        'Tabloid',
    ];

    /**
     * Maximum allowed filename length.
     */
    private const MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH = 100;

    /**
     * Path to the wkhtmltopdf binary (absolute path to avoid PATH manipulation).
     */
    private const WKHTMLTOPDF_BINARY = '/usr/local/bin/wkhtmltopdf';

    /**
     * Generate a PDF report using wkhtmltopdf.
     *
     * @param Request $request
     * @return \Illuminate\Http\Response|\Symfony\Component\HttpFoundation\BinaryFileResponse
     */
    public function generate(Request $request)
    {
        $request->validate([
            'size' => ['required', 'string'],
            'filename' => ['required', 'string', 'max:' . self::MAX_FILENAME_LENGTH],
            'html_content' => ['required', 'string'],
        ]);

        $size = $request->input('size');
        $filename = $request->input('filename');
        $htmlContent = $request->input('html_content');

        // Validate page size against allowlist
        if (!in_array($size, self::ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES, true)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid page size. Allowed sizes: ' . implode(', ', self::ALLOWED_PAGE_SIZES),
            ], 422);
        }

        // Sanitize filename: allow only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores
        $sanitizedFilename = preg_replace('/[^a-zA-Z0-9_\-]/', '', $filename);

        if (empty($sanitizedFilename)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'Invalid filename. Only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores are allowed.',
            ], 422);
        }

        // Verify the wkhtmltopdf binary exists and is executable
        if (!is_file(self::WKHTMLTOPDF_BINARY) || !is_executable(self::WKHTMLTOPDF_BINARY)) {
            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'PDF generation tool is not available on this server.',
            ], 500);
        }

        // Create temporary files with secure paths
        $tempDir = storage_path('app/temp');
        if (!is_dir($tempDir)) {
            mkdir($tempDir, 0750, true);
        }

        $uniqueId = bin2hex(random_bytes(16));
        $inputPath = $tempDir . '/' . $uniqueId . '_input.html';
        $outputPath = $tempDir . '/' . $sanitizedFilename . '_' . $uniqueId . '.pdf';

        try {
            // Write HTML content to a temporary file
            file_put_contents($inputPath, $htmlContent);

            // Use Symfony Process component to avoid shell injection entirely.
            // Each argument is passed as a separate array element — no shell interpolation occurs.
            $process = new Process([
                self::WKHTMLTOPDF_BINARY,
                '--page-size',
                $size,
                '--disable-local-file-access',
                '--disable-javascript',
                '--no-stop-slow-scripts',
                '--quiet',
                $inputPath,
                $outputPath,
            ]);

            $process->setTimeout(30);
            $process->run();

            if (!$process->isSuccessful()) {
                // Log the error but don't expose internal details to the user
                \Log::error('wkhtmltopdf failed', [
                    'exit_code' => $process->getExitCode(),
                    'error_output' => $process->getErrorOutput(),
                ]);

                return response()->json([
                    'error' => 'Failed to generate PDF report.',
                ], 500);
            }

            if (!file_exists($outputPath)) {
                return response()->json([
                    'error' => 'PDF file was not created.',
                ], 500);
            }

            // Return the PDF as a download, then clean up
            return response()
                ->download($outputPath, $sanitizedFilename . '.pdf', [
                    'Content-Type' => 'application/pdf',
                ])
                ->deleteFileAfterSend(true);

        } catch (ProcessFailedException $e) {
            \Log::error('PDF generation process failed', [
                'message' => $e->getMessage(),
            ]);

            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'An error occurred during PDF generation.',
            ], 500);

        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            \Log::error('Unexpected error during PDF generation', [
                'message' => $e->getMessage(),
            ]);

            return response()->json([
                'error' => 'An unexpected error occurred.',
            ], 500);

        } finally {
            // Always clean up the input file
            if (file_exists($inputPath)) {
                unlink($inputPath);
            }
        }
    }
}
